title: RHCSA 10 Lab 31: Systemd Timer
description: Create and enable a persistent systemd timer

tasks: On server, create /usr/local/sbin/rhcsa10-timer.sh so it appends TIMER OK to /var/log/rhcsa10-timer.log.
On server, create a oneshot service named rhcsa10-timer.service that runs /usr/local/sbin/rhcsa10-timer.sh.
On server, create rhcsa10-timer.timer so it runs every 5 minutes, is persistent, and starts automatically at boot.
hints: Enable the timer unit, not the service unit.
Use OnCalendar and Persistent in the timer unit.
checks: # server systemctl cat rhcsa10-timer.service | grep -Fqx 'ExecStart=/usr/local/sbin/rhcsa10-timer.sh' && systemctl cat rhcsa10-timer.service | grep -Fqx 'Type=oneshot'

# server systemctl cat rhcsa10-timer.timer | grep -Fqx 'OnCalendar=*:0/5' && systemctl cat rhcsa10-timer.timer | grep -Fqx 'Persistent=true' && systemctl is-enabled rhcsa10-timer.timer | grep -qx enabled
solution_outline: Make the requested configuration persistent and verify it before finishing.
